01_initial_request.input_data: SARS-CoV-2 spike protein, viral escape mutation data, and host molecules
01_initial_request.user_intent: To engineer therapeutic antibodies that overcome antibody escape mutations and reduce disease severity
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Reduce viral load or replication and alleviate dysregulation of the host immune response

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] SARS-CoV-2 spike protein mediates viral entry via ACE2 binding. [Gap] Conventional antibodies targeting variable regions face escape mutations. [Decision] To achieve viral entry blockade, chose to use Monoclonal Antibody Development to perform targeting on Spike Protein (RBD/NTD), expecting to obtain inhibition of viral attachment.

parameters.mechanism: Block ACE2 binding or steric hindrance

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Therapeutic conventional antibodies constitute a significant group of FDA-approved SARS-CoV-2 antibodies; blocks viral attachment or entry.

references.citation: Westendorf K, et al. LY-CoV1404 (bebtelovimab) potently neutralizes SARS-CoV-2 variants. Cell Rep. 2022
references.relevance: Demonstrates efficacy of anti-spike monoclonal antibodies.

references.citation: Huang Y, et al. Structural and functional properties of SARS-CoV-2 spike protein. Acta Pharmacol Sin. 2020
references.relevance: Describes spike protein structure and antibody targets.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High mutation rates in Spike protein (e.g., Omicron) cause loss of antibody activity. [Gap] Available antibodies become obsolete due to escape mutations. [Decision] To achieve cross-variant protection, chose to use Epitope Mapping to perform selection of pancoronavirus conserved epitopes, expecting to obtain resistance to viral escape.

parameters.target_region: Conserved RBD epitopes, non-RBM part of RBD, S2 subunit

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Antibodies binding to conserved epitopes (e.g., Bebtelovimab, S2H97) demonstrated cross-variant protection including Omicron.

references.citation: Starr TN, et al. SARS-CoV-2 RBD antibodies that maximize breadth and resistance to escape. Nature. 2021
references.relevance: Identifies antibodies maximizing breadth and resistance to escape.

references.citation: Wang L, et al. Immune evasion of neutralizing antibodies by SARS-CoV-2 Omicron. Cytokine Growth Factor Rev. 2023
references.relevance: Details mutation impact on antibody activity.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fc functions affect antibody half-life and immune activation (ADE risk). [Gap] Need to prolong half-life and reduce immunopathology. [Decision] To achieve optimized pharmacokinetics and safety, chose to use Site-Directed Mutagenesis to perform Fc Engineering (YTE, LALA, TM mutations), expecting to obtain increased serum half-life and reduced Fc effector function.

parameters.goal: Increase affinity for FcRn or decrease binding to Fc receptors

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutations increased serum half-life (long-acting) and diminished Fc effector function in vitro, lowering risk of Fc-mediated lung injury.

references.citation: Loo YM, et al. The SARS-CoV-2 monoclonal antibody combination, AZD7442, is protective in nonhuman primates and has an extended half-life in humans. Sci Transl Med. 2022
references.relevance: Demonstrates extended half-life with YTE mutations.

references.citation: Schlothauer T, et al. Novel human IgG1 and IgG4 Fc-engineered antibodies with completely abolished immune effector functions. Protein Eng Des Sel. 2016
references.relevance: Describes Fc mutations abolishing effector functions.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Conventional antibodies have Fc-associated ADE risks and large size. [Gap] Need better tissue penetration and reduced ADE. [Decision] To achieve enhanced stability and penetration, chose to use Nanobody Isolation to perform development of single-domain antibodies (sdAb), expecting to obtain small, stable molecules without Fc portion.

parameters.characteristics: Long CDR3, size ten times lower than conventional antibodies
parameters.stability_conditions: Acidic, ionic strength, heat, proteolysis, pH

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Nanobodies facilitate access to hidden epitopes, are stable in harsh environments, and reduce risk of Fc-associated ADE.

references.citation: Xu J, et al. Nanobodies from camelid mice and llamas neutralize SARS-CoV-2 variants. Nature. 2021
references.relevance: Reports neutralization of variants by nanobodies.

references.citation: Wrapp D, et al. Structural basis for potent neutralization of betacoronaviruses by single-domain camelid antibodies. Cell. 2020
references.relevance: Describes structural basis of nanobody neutralization.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Single epitope targeting allows viral escape. [Gap] Need improved avidity and escape resistance. [Decision] To achieve multispecificity, chose to use Protein Fusion/Linker Design to perform construction of Bispecific/Multivalent Antibodies, expecting to obtain increased neutralization potency and resistance to mutational escape.

parameters.format: Bi-, tri-, and multivalent antibody formats
parameters.linkage: Fc dimerization, peptide linker, self-assembly protein scaffold (e.g., apoferritin, p53 tetramerizing domain)
parameters.specificity: Different epitopes of spike protein or viral/host molecules

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Bispecific antibodies increased neutralization potency and resistance to mutational escape; multibody overcomes point mutation.

references.citation: De Gasparo R, et al. Bispecific IgG neutralizes SARS-CoV-2 variants and prevents escape in mice. Nature. 2021
references.relevance: Demonstrates prevention of escape with bispecific IgG.

references.citation: Rujas E, et al. Multivalency transforms SARS-CoV-2 antibodies into ultrapotent neutralizers. Nat Commun. 2021

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hyperinflammation (cytokine storm) causes disease severity. [Gap] Need to alleviate dysregulation of host immune response. [Decision] To achieve immunomodulation, chose to use Antibody Repurposing to perform targeting of Host Molecules (IL-6R, CD147), expecting to obtain reduced inflammation and disease severity.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Tocilizumab reduced disease severity and hospitalization time (mortality effect inconclusive); Meplazumab improved severity and mortality while reducing viral load.

references.citation: Wang K, et al. CD147-spike protein is a novel route for SARS-CoV-2 infection to host cells. Signal Transduct Target Ther. 2020
references.relevance: Identifies CD147 as a receptor and target for repurposing.

references.citation: Salama C, et al. Tocilizumab in patients hospitalized with Covid-19 pneumonia. N Engl J Med. 2021

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Clinical Trials and Preclinical In Vitro/In Vivo Assays

antibody_activity_loss.interpretation: Decrease in activity of available anti-spike antibody due to amino acid substitutions (e.g., S477N, T478K).

hospitalization_time.interpretation: Tocilizumab found to reduce disease severity and hospitalization time.

mortality_and_severity.interpretation: Meplazumab effectively improving disease severity and mortality while reducing viral load.

half_life_extension.interpretation: Fc mutations (YTE) resulted in prolonged circulating IgG levels.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Engineered therapeutic antibodies targeting conserved epitopes, utilizing Fc engineering, and employing bispecific formats successfully overcome antibody escape mutations and reduce disease severity, though host-targeting immunomodulators show controversial mortality outcomes.
